title: feat(routing): provide a mechanism to synchronize the search
body: This PR provides a new implementation API for a URL Synchronisation. This PR is followup on the PoC done here: https://github.com/algolia/instantsearch.js/pull/2636 # Motivation The previous system for implementing the synchronization with the URL `urlSync` has several issues: - it relies on concepts from the helper which are not friendly enough because the user will be at the very beginning of their learning of the Algolia JS Stack - it is not customizable enough: users don't have a complete control over the form of the URL (can't change the path) or the display of the filters # Solution This new implementation is based on the idea that a better way of thinking about the state of the search with InstantSearch is based on the UI and more specifically the widgets. Here the widgets will be responsible to provide their view of the search state so that it can be transformed (with stateMappings) and then serialized (with routers) # Benefits - default values are not serialized - customization of the URL (path) - pluggable output - mapping / simplification of the parameters in the output using mappings - widget based serialized UI state # API ## Core elements - RoutingManager: widget that handles the sync of the widgets and the storage (hiden inside the core of InstantSearch) - Router: parts that actually writes / reads and listen to the storage - StateMapping: adapter between the UI state and the datastructure saved in the router ## IS.js library API - routing: boolean | {} - router: Router instance - stateMapping: StateMapping instance ## new Widget methods - getWidgetState: `(fullState: UIState, {state: SearchParameters, helper: Helper}) => UIState` - getWidgetSearchParameters: `(searchParam: SearchParameters, {uiState: UIState}) => SearchParameters` Should be mindful about new objects (no new unnecessary new objects). Should be aware of empty values and default values as they should not be present in the UIState. ```javascript getWidgetState(fullState, { state }) { return { ...fullState, /* sub UI state for the widget here */ }; }, getWidgetSearchParameters(searchParam, { uiState }) { // Modify and return the searchParam based on uiState // return searchParam.setQueryParameter('page', 0); }, ``` ## Router interface - read() - write() - onUpdate - createURL - dispose() ## StateMapping API - stateToRoute: SearchParameters => object - routeToState: object => SearchParameters ## Namespaces (and bundled implementations) - instantsearch.**routers**.history() : url based - instantsearch.**stateMappings**.simple() : ## History Router API (this has to be instanciated, and there are specific options that can customized) - windowTitle: () => string - writeDelay: number - createURL: (qsModule, object) => string - parseURL: (qsModule, location) => object ## Simple mapping No API # Overview # Demos (from the previous PR) - [with default implementations based on modern history API](https://disastrous-approval.surge.sh) ([source](http://disastrous-approval.surge.sh/app.js)) - [With non-trivial mappings](http://weary-plants.surge.sh/Cameras%20&%20Camcorders/Binoculars?brand=Canon), brand refinement list data is mapped to brand query string parameter and the hierarchical menu is mapped to the path name of the URL - ([source](http://weary-plants.surge.sh/app.js)) - [start page count at 1 in the URL](http://nebulous-balls.surge.sh/?page=3) - ([Source](http://nebulous-balls.surge.sh/app.js)) # Next steps (in new PR's) - Find a place in the reference documentation - Test the core of the feature - See if we miss some feature - Write a thorough guide on how to use the feature # Reviews - [x] hierarchical-menu/ - [x] hits-per-page/ - [x] menu/ - [x] numeric-refinement-list/ - [x] numeric-selector/ - [x] pagination/ - [x] price-ranges/ - [x] range/ - [x] refinement-list/ - [x] search-box/ - [x] sort-by-selector/ - [x] star-rating/ - [x] toggle/ - [x] instantsearch.js + router + stateMappings ## Guidelines for reviewing (as a team) The list just above will let us track what has been reviewed :) For the review of the widgets, there are two main elements to check: - the tests, they will also give an idea of the kind of the behavior we expect (see spec above). There is a part of copy paste there, so be sure to check if the descriptions are good too. - the widget implementation. See if it complies to the spec and check if you see some **meaningful** improvements to be done. The core part is not tested in this PR. But the code is ready 😁 Check if you see some dead code that was backported from the previous implementation for no reason. Check if you see some meaningful improvements. This part should be jsdocced so see if it's ok.
